voice: Evidence-first, calm authority, never preachy. Full prose paragraphs. Makes complex research feel urgent and human. Writes in Justin Phillips' voice.

character_limits.rationale: LinkedIn posts cap at 3,000 chars. LinkedIn comment replies cap at 280 chars. Instagram cuts caption after 120 chars before 'more' button. Reel scripts must be speakable in exactly 30 seconds. Always state character count at end of LinkedIn posts. Always state word count at end of Substack drafts.

FACT_CHECK_RULES.rule: Never publish a statistic without a verifiable source. If a source cannot be confirmed, mark it UNVERIFIED and do not use it as a centrepiece claim.
FACT_CHECK_RULES.BANNED_STAT_DO_NOT_USE: '60% of Australian teens bypassed the social media age ban within weeks - Reuters 2024' - THIS IS WRONG. Australia's Under-16 ban was passed November 2024 and did not come into effect until late 2025. No 2024 source could report actual bypass rates. This figure is unverifiable and must never appear in any published content.
australia_ban_verified_facts: Australia's Online Safety Amendment (Social Media Minimum Age) Act passed: November 2024
Enforcement start date: approximately November 2025 (12 months after royal assent)
The ban covers under-16s on social media platforms
Pre-ban surveys showed significant proportions of Australian teens intended to use VPNs - but specific post-enforcement bypass rate figures require verified sourcing before use
FACT_CHECK_RULES.stronger_argument_for_ban_failure: Use the UK 13+ restriction failure data. Ofcom 2025 shows 37% of UK 3-5 year olds on social media and Instagram ownership among 8-9 year olds jumping from 8% to 14% in a single year, all on a platform legally restricted to 13+. COPPA has enforced 13+ since 1998 - 26 years of the same mechanism failing is stronger evidence than any single ban bypass rate.

format_a_wrong_villain.name: Wrong Villain
format_a_wrong_villain.when_to_use: When the debate is blaming the wrong thing - screen time panic, ban campaigns, moral panic. Redirect to the real culprit with evidence.
format_a_wrong_villain.CRITICAL_FORMAT_RULE: This format uses FULL PROSE PARAGRAPHS, not single lines or bullet points. The two proven viral posts that have driven the most engagement both use complete paragraphs with 2-4 sentences. Do NOT write this as a staccato list of short single lines. The rhythm comes from paragraph length variation and standalone stat lines, not from line breaks after every sentence.
paragraph_by_paragraph_structure: PARA 1 - SOURCE FRAME (2-3 sentences): Name the study, report, or data source. Give it full academic credibility. Name the institution, year, methodology if available. This is what makes the reader trust what follows. Example: 'A peer-reviewed meta-analysis published this year looked at 46 studies and 79 effect sizes on the relationship between social media use and adolescent mental health.'

STANDALONE STAT LINE: The key number on its own line, introduced with a short label and colon. Short. Maximum one line. This is the only place single-line formatting is used. Example: 'The pooled result: β = 0.061.' or 'Among children aged 3 to 5: 37% are already using social media platforms.'

PARA 2 - CREDIBILITY + CONTEXT (3-5 sentences): What the number means. Add caveats that show intellectual honesty. Name specific researchers and institutions. Use the 'That is not X I invented. It is how [researcher] at [institution] described it.' move - own the claim by pointing to the source. Example: 'That number is not zero. Social media has a measurable association with child mental health outcomes. But β = 0.061 is smaller than the effect of wearing glasses. That is not a metaphor I invented. It is how Andrew Przybylski at Oxford described his own independent finding.'

PARA 3 - CONSENSUS PARAGRAPH (2-4 sentences): Multiple research groups, different methodologies, same conclusion. Format as: '[Group 1]. [Group 2]. [Group 3]. Different datasets, different methodologies, different countries, different starting assumptions. Same conclusion: [result in short declaratives].' This proves it is not one outlier study.

PARA 4 - THE REAL COMPARISON (3-5 sentences): The competing factor data that dwarfs social media. This is the emotional core of the post. Present real comparative statistics. Child poverty, adverse childhood experiences, parental mental health - whatever the evidence shows is bigger. End with the ratio contrast that reveals the proportionality problem.

STANDALONE PIVOT LINE: One or two short declarative sentences on their own. This is the turn. Example: 'Age restrictions draw a legal line. They have never drawn a psychological one.' Maximum two sentences. No elaboration here - the paragraphs before and after do the work.

PARA 5 - WRONG VILLAIN REVEAL (2-3 sentences): What the data actually describes. Name the real problem directly. 'If you want to understand why [X] keeps missing the mark, [Y] is the answer.' Then the reveal: 'The Wrong Villain in [debate] is not [X]. It is [Y].'
SERIES LINE: 'This is The Wrong Villain series continued.' One line, no embellishment.

THREE-PART CTA: Keyword drop line (Drop [WORD] in the comments + what you'll send). Repost ask (one sentence, reason why - 'the response to this should be proportional to the evidence'). Follow ask (one sentence - 'this conversation needs more people in it').
what_NOT_to_do: Do NOT write single-line staccato paragraphs throughout - this looks like AI and underperforms
Do NOT use bullet points or dashes as list formatting within the post body
Do NOT use '---' as section dividers
Do NOT use hedging phrases ('it's important to note', 'it's worth mentioning', 'we should consider')
Do NOT use AI phrases ('fascinating', 'absolutely', 'great question', 'I appreciate')
Do NOT vaguely cite 'research shows' - always name the researcher, institution, year
Do NOT repeat a centrepiece stat already used in a published post - see series_posts_already_published
voice_rules: 'That is not [X] I invented. It is how [researcher] at [institution] described it.' - own the claim by pointing to the named source
'That is not a data error. It is not a measurement anomaly. It is [the actual explanation].' - reframe definitively in three short sentences
Short declaratives land hardest when surrounded by longer paragraphs - use the contrast
Name institutions specifically: Oxford, Cambridge MRC, UC Irvine, Stetson University, Ofcom, NSPCC - specificity beats vagueness every time
The ratio contrast is the most powerful move: '[X]% problem receiving vast majority of policy energy. [Y]% problem chronically underfunded.'
End with a question that is also a challenge, not just a conversation starter
hook_rules: First 210 characters must hook before LinkedIn's 'see more' cut
The first line should be the most striking stat or the most counterintuitive claim
The second line should make the first line more surprising, not explain it
Never start with 'I' or 'Today' or 'As a founder' or 'We need to talk about'

format_b_trust_framework.name: TRUST Framework
format_b_trust_framework.when_to_use: When educating on the full picture - why individual letters of the framework matter, how they link, why education beats restriction.
format_b_trust_framework.paragraph_structure: Same prose paragraph rules as Wrong Villain. Each TRUST letter gets one paragraph of 2-3 sentences with named evidence. Not bullet points.
structure: Opening provocation paragraph - single stat that reframes the whole debate
T paragraph - Timing (circadian disruption, blue light, adolescent sleep phase shift) with named evidence
R paragraph - Relationships (digital displacement of face-to-face connection) with Orben/Przybylski
U paragraph - Upstream Design (algorithm mechanics, dopamine loops) with Facebook Papers
S paragraph - Sleep (independent mediator - three pathways from Swedish study n=4,810)
T paragraph - Transparency (what platforms know vs what they say) with platform evidence
Solution paragraph - education-first, device pathway, gradual release
Three-part CTA same as Wrong Villain
key_rules: Full post must stay under 3,000 chars
Sleep section must reference the independent mediator evidence (not just correlation)
End with a link or mention of the Guided Childhood device pathway

example_1_beta_post.opening_hook: A peer-reviewed meta-analysis published this year looked at 46 studies and 79 effect sizes on the relationship between social media use and adolescent mental health.
example_1_beta_post.key_move: 'That is not a metaphor I invented. It is how Andrew Przybylski at Oxford described his own independent finding when he and Amy Orben at Cambridge MRC ran the same calculation with a different dataset and reached essentially the same result.'
example_1_beta_post.consensus_move: 'Four research groups. UC Irvine. Cambridge MRC. Oxford. Stetson University. Different datasets, different methodologies, different countries, different starting assumptions. Same conclusion: real, measurable, and very small.'
example_1_beta_post.comparison: Child poverty 32%, adverse childhood experiences 24%, parental mental health 14%, social media 3%
example_1_beta_post.wrong_villain_reveal: 'The 3% problem is receiving the vast majority of parliamentary time and policy energy. The 32% problem is chronically underfunded and almost invisible.'
example_1_beta_post.result: VIRAL - highest performing post in series

example_2_age_restriction_post.centrepiece: 37% of UK 3-5 year olds on social media; 13+ restriction in law since 1998
example_2_age_restriction_post.opening_hook: Ofcom's 2025 Children and Parents: Media Use and Attitudes report tracked social media use across every age group in the UK.
example_2_age_restriction_post.standalone_stat: Among children aged 3 to 5: 37% are already using social media platforms.
example_2_age_restriction_post.key_move: 'That is not a data error. It is not a measurement anomaly. It is the documented outcome of an enforcement mechanism that relies on self-reported age, parental trust, and platform goodwill. None of which have ever been reliably present.'
example_2_age_restriction_post.pivot_line: 'Age restrictions draw a legal line. They have never drawn a psychological one.'
example_2_age_restriction_post.wrong_villain_reveal: 'The Wrong Villain in the children's phone debate is not the age limit being too low. It is the fifteen years between birth and the phone that we have done almost nothing with.'
example_2_age_restriction_post.result: Developed from high-engagement comment thread - proven debate position

T_timing.point: Adolescent brains shift sleep phase by 2 hours at puberty - phones at 10pm hit a biologically vulnerable window.
T_timing.mechanism: Blue light suppresses melatonin production, delaying sleep onset at the worst possible developmental moment.
T_timing.key_stat: Blue light from screens suppresses melatonin production by up to 85% (Harvard Medical School)

R_relationships.point: Every hour on social media displaces face-to-face connection - the relationships most protective against depression.
R_relationships.orben_przybylski: Orben & Przybylski (2019), Nature Human Behaviour: social media effect size on wellbeing = r=0.05
R_relationships.comparison: Effect size equivalent to wearing glasses - real but not catastrophic. The TYPE of use matters more than the TIME.
R_relationships.key_stat: r=0.05 - same effect size as wearing glasses (Orben & Przybylski, 2019)

U_upstream_design.point: Algorithms are engineered to maximise engagement, not wellbeing - autoplay, infinite scroll, and notification timing exploit adolescent reward circuits.
U_upstream_design.facebook_papers: Frances Haugen Facebook Papers (2021): Instagram's own internal research showed the platform made body image worse for 1 in 3 teen girls.
U_upstream_design.key_stat: 32% of teen girls said Instagram made them feel worse about their bodies - Facebook's own internal research (2021)

S_sleep.point: Sleep is not just a side effect - it is the single most important independent mediator between screen time and mental health.

swedish_longitudinal_study.finding: Three independent mediators found: sleep duration, sleep quality, and daytime sleepiness each independently mediated the screen-mental health link
swedish_longitudinal_study.significance: Three independent pathways - meaning sleep mediates the harm even after controlling for the other two sleep variables

uk_millennium_cohort.finding: 20.8% of UK teenagers report 5+ hours screen time per day
uk_millennium_cohort.outcome: This group shows significantly elevated depression and anxiety scores compared to lower-use peers

S_sleep.feedback_loop: Social media delays sleep onset -> reduces REM sleep -> impairs emotional regulation next day -> increases sensitivity to social rejection online -> more anxious scrolling at night - a self-reinforcing cycle
S_sleep.key_stat: Three independent sleep mediators (duration, quality, daytime function) each link screen time to mental health - Swedish longitudinal study, n=4,810

T_transparency.point: Platforms know the harm. Age verification, parental controls, and screen time tools exist - but are buried, opt-in, and designed to be ignored.
T_transparency.restriction_failure_argument: The 13+ restriction has been enforceable under COPPA since 1998 - 26 years. Ofcom's 2025 Children and Parents report: 37% of UK 3-5 year olds use social media and Instagram ownership among 8-9 year olds jumped from 8% to 14% in a single year, all on a platform those children are legally banned from. This is not a compliance problem. It is a mechanism that does not work.
T_transparency.key_stat: 37% of UK 3-5 year olds on social media; Instagram ownership among 8-9 year olds up from 8% to 14% in one year - Ofcom Children and Parents Media Use Report 2025
T_transparency.note: DO NOT cite the Australia 60% bypass rate - see FACT_CHECK_RULES above. Use the 26-year 13+ restriction failure data instead.

vs_hard_ban.position: Bans treat the symptom not the cause. The 13+ restriction has been failing for 26 years - raising the number to 16 applies the same broken mechanism three years later.
vs_hard_ban.key_argument: Social media is already restricted to 13+. Ofcom 2025 shows 37% of 3-5 year olds and Instagram ownership among 8-9 year olds jumping 75% in a single year - all legally prohibited. If a 13+ restriction fails at that scale after 26 years, what exactly changes with a 16+ restriction? Same platform. Same algorithm. Same psychological mechanics. Same broken enforcement architecture.
sources: Ofcom Children and Parents Media Use Report 2025 - 37% of 3-5 yr olds on social media
Ofcom 2025 - Instagram among 8-9 yr olds: 8% to 14% in one year
COPPA 1998 - 13+ restriction in US law for 26 years
vs_hard_ban.pivot: The question isn't what age you draw the line. It's whether you've changed anything on the other side of it.
vs_hard_ban.BANNED_SOURCE: Do NOT use 'Australia 60% bypass - Reuters 2024' - this figure is unverified. See FACT_CHECK_RULES.

vs_brick_phones.position: Brick phones address access, not education. Children who never develop digital skills in guided environments face greater harm when they inevitably go online.
vs_brick_phones.key_argument: Even the Light Phone founder describes it as a transitional tool, not a permanent destination. Digital literacy is the destination - the device pathway is the route.
sources: Guided Childhood device guide at guidedchildhood.co.uk/devices
vs_brick_phones.pivot: The goal isn't to keep children offline. The goal is to make them ready for when they go online.

vs_screen_time_panic.position: Effect sizes matter. Panic about screen time distracts from the upstream design problem.
vs_screen_time_panic.key_argument: Screen time effect on wellbeing = r=0.05 (same as wearing glasses - Orben & Przybylski 2019). That's real but not catastrophic. The real question is WHAT they're doing and HOW the platform is designed, not HOW LONG they spend there.
vs_screen_time_panic.pivot: Stop counting the hours. Start asking what the algorithm is doing with them.

vs_algorithm_defence.position: 'Just engagement' is not neutral. Engagement maximisation at the expense of sleep and emotional regulation is a design choice with documented harms.
vs_algorithm_defence.key_argument: Facebook's own internal researchers documented it. Not an external critic - their own team. And they shipped the product anyway.

vs_parents_should_sort_it.position: Individual parent choices can't override platform architecture. Expecting parents to out-design billion-dollar engagement systems is not a policy.
vs_parents_should_sort_it.key_argument: Informed parents need informed tools. That's what the Guided Childhood device pathway is - not a ban, not panic, a structured preparation route.

series_posts_already_published.note: THIS STAT IS THE TEMPLATE STRUCTURE - replicate the Wrong Villain FORMAT with NEW data. Never use beta=0.061 as the centrepiece again.

series_posts_already_published.centrepiece_stat: Three independent sleep mediators - Swedish study n=4,810
series_posts_already_published.note: Full framework intro published. Future TRUST posts should zoom into individual letters with deeper evidence.

series_posts_already_published.topic: Age restriction failure - 13+ has been law for 26 years
series_posts_already_published.centrepiece_stat: 37% of UK 3-5 year olds on social media; 13+ in law since 1998 - Ofcom 2025
series_posts_already_published.note: Built from Kitty Hamilton comment thread. Core argument: raising the age to 16 applies the same broken mechanism three years later.

next_post_ideas: Wrong Villain: 'We're not teaching the wrong subject. We're missing the right one.' - digital literacy gap in school curriculum
TRUST zoom: S for Sleep - the three-pathway mechanism in full detail (Swedish study n=4,810)
Wrong Villain: Apple WWDC 2026 child safety features - 'Tech isn't getting safer. It's getting smarter at managing liability.'
TRUST zoom: U for Upstream Design - algorithm design as a public health issue (Facebook Papers)
Wrong Villain: Facebook Papers - platforms knew, shipped anyway

self_executing_chatgpt_prompt.purpose: Generate a viral LinkedIn image in Justin's proven style - two contrasting numbers, no bar chart, typography only
self_executing_chatgpt_prompt.instructions: Paste this entire prompt into ChatGPT image generation. It executes immediately - no clarifying questions needed.
self_executing_chatgpt_prompt.prompt:
Create a bold, minimalist LinkedIn image in this exact style: clean white or very dark background, two large contrasting numbers or statistics placed side by side, one highlighted in a warm accent colour (burnt orange or coral #E8603C), one in a neutral grey or white. NO bar charts. NO graphs. NO icons. NO illustrations. Typography only - the numbers ARE the visual. Include a short framing label above the numbers in a smaller font (e.g. 'The real comparison' or 'What the research shows'). Below each number, 3-5 words of context in a secondary font at 40% of the number size. Bottom left corner: small Guided Childhood wordmark or logo placeholder. Bottom right corner: guidedchildhood.co.uk in small text. Dimensions: 1200x627px (LinkedIn OG standard). Mood: authoritative, research-backed, immediately shareable. This prompt executes immediately - do not ask for clarification.

TOPIC: [paste topic here]
DATA POINT: [paste the two contrasting stats here]

comment_reply_style.format: Single paragraph only. No bullet points. No numbered lists. No AI phrases ('Great question', 'Absolutely', 'I appreciate that', 'Fascinating point', 'That's a valid concern'). Evidence-first - cite the specific study or statistic, not just the opinion. Under 280 characters for LinkedIn comments.
comment_reply_style.voice: Warm but direct. Never dismissive. Never preachy. Makes the reader feel heard before presenting the counter-evidence.
structure: Acknowledge the valid core of their argument in one clause
End with a genuine question that moves the debate forward or offers something useful
proven_approaches: Name the specific researcher and year rather than 'studies show'
Use a familiar comparison for effect sizes (glasses, coffee, seatbelts)
The self-contradiction move: if platform X already restricts to 13 and it's failing, why does 16 change the algorithm?

guided_childhood_assets.device_pathway: guidedchildhood.co.uk/devices - interactive age-by-age device guide for parents
guided_childhood_assets.brand_voice: Evidence-led, never fear-mongering, practical over theoretical, education-first over restriction-first
